question: Will the US attack Iran's infrastructure before June 1st?
textDescription:
This market resolves to YES if, at any point before June 1st, 2026 (UTC), the United States government or military conducts a direct military strike against Iranian infrastructure. "Infrastructure" is defined as fixed, non purely military, facilities, including but not limited to oil refineries, nuclear facilities, power grids, or transportation

If no such strike occurs by the specified time, or if the strike is against military sites, or the action is conducted by a third party (e.g., Israel or an insurgent group) without direct U.S. military involvement, the market resolves to NO.

Resolution will be based on reports from reputable international news outlets (e.g., Reuters, Associated Press, BBC).
newsKeyEvidence: U.S. military conducted airstrikes in southern Iran on May 25, 2026, targeting missile sites and boats. | Iran condemned the U.S. strikes as a display of 'bad faith' amid ongoing negotiations. | Diplomatic talks are ongoing, with Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir in Tehran to mediate a peace deal. | President Trump postponed a planned military strike on Iran at the request of Gulf allies. | The U.S. military conducted 'self-defense' strikes in southern Iran on May 25, 2026, targeting missile launch sites and boats. | Iran condemned the U.S. airstrikes as a violation of good faith amid ongoing negotiations. | Diplomatic talks are ongoing, with Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir in Tehran to mediate a peace deal. | President Trump postponed a planned military strike on Iran at the request of Gulf allies.
